document_summary: Sohu news report covering the final review process of the 13th Tiger Roar Awards. Details the judging process involving 92 final review judges, 416 shortlisted companies, and over 1,000 case works. Describes the appointment ceremony, presidium meeting, panel discussion, and professional standards. Supports Liu's participation in this judging process as Deputy Director of the Organizing Committee.

snippets.text: In the past weekend, a "crucial battle" exclusively for 92 final review judges, 416 shortlisted companies and over 1,000 selected case works of Tiger Roar Award went quietly, confidentially, intensely and smoothly.
snippets.subject: Tiger Roar Awards
snippets.reasoning: Establishes the scale and rigor of the judging process: 92 final judges, 416 companies, 1000+ cases. Liu was part of this process as Deputy Director.

snippets.text: Although the final review of this Tiger Roar Award was held online, the review process, competition rules and panel discussion requirements were successively carried out in a rigorous, strict and serious manner, mainly including meeting of the presidium, opening ceremony, online final review, panel discussion and other key processes.
snippets.subject: Tiger Roar Awards
snippets.reasoning: Describes the rigorous multi-step review process that judges including Liu participated in.

snippets.text: In terms of the mode, 92 judges for final review gathered online, with uniform background, and accepted appointment one by one in an orderly and harmonious manner; in terms of content, the appointment sequence of the final review was subject to appointment of the presidium - appointment of the supervision and examination committee - appointment of judges for group final review. It not only broke the barrier of mode from body and mind to the largest extent and closed the distance among the judges, but also thoroughly integrated the scientific and rigorous principles of review for Tiger Roar Award and vitalized the whole online final review.
snippets.subject: Tiger Roar Awards
snippets.reasoning: Details the formal appointment ceremony for all 92 judges and the hierarchical appointment structure, demonstrating the professional standards of the judging process.

snippets.text: Later, Xiaodong Zheng made a detailed instruction on the collection of the awards. He mentioned that the \(13^{\mathrm{th}}\) Tiger Roar Award altogether received more than 4,000 case works of various categories (with a year- on- year growth of \(22\%\) ) from more than 800 participating companies (with a year- on- year growth of \(17\%\) ). Over \(50\%\) 's participating companies were selected out for final review upon online review, to be specific, 416 companies. As to the whole case works, more than 1,000 works were selected out for final review, with the proportion of finalists of case works not higher than \(27.5\%\) of the total cases.
snippets.subject: Tiger Roar Awards
snippets.reasoning: Key quantitative data: 4000+ case works, 800+ companies, 22% YoY growth, 27.5% finalist rate. Demonstrates the massive scale and selectivity of the awards process.

snippets.text: "Judges for the final review of Tiger Roar Award are benchmark and leading figures of the digital marketing industry. Based on the review of this competition, on the one hand, I hope you can find quality content from the cases, and explore methodology leading the healthy development of the industry; on the other hand, I hope you could have a restricted view of the review to think on what every digital personnel could do in the rapid development of digital economy, and rethink on what kind of energy digital marketing could bring to the digital economy when getting back into the routine."
snippets.subject: Tiger Roar Awards judges
snippets.reasoning: Chairman Xisha Li's statement that Tiger Roar judges are 'benchmark and leading figures of the digital marketing industry' establishes the prestige of the judging role Liu held.

snippets.text: Upon give- and- back, heated debate, online frequent sharing and mutual persuasion, this year's nine groups of judges finally reached consensus on the strict norms of scoring standards and weigh, reduction and observance of proportion of level awards for various case categories, qualification examination and strict examination for competition participation, further making the review of Tiger Roar Award more professional, more objective and more scientific, and making the winning of Tiger Roar Award more solid, with higher weight and value.
snippets.subject: Tiger Roar Awards
snippets.reasoning: Describes the rigorous panel discussion process with nine groups of judges reaching consensus on strict scoring standards.

snippets.text: Tiger Roar Award is the comprehensive award of the most professional influence in the digitization of Chinese brand marketing. Guided by the Digital Marketing Committee of China Advertising Association of Commerce, and adhering to the purpose of "being building personnel of the industry", Tiger Roar Award is constantly outputting various kinds of value contents for the industry and brand enterprises.
snippets.subject: Tiger Roar Awards
snippets.reasoning: Establishes Tiger Roar Award as 'the comprehensive award of the most professional influence in the digitization of Chinese brand marketing'.

snippets.text: Tiger Roar Award has a large jury made up of more than 600 well- known practical experts in the industry. Upon online review, offline final review, defense for gold award and other rigorous and normative review process, it always maintains a gold award winning rate of \(1\%\) . Its principle of putting quality before quantity and ensuring value has selected out and recorded excellent cases of each year for the industry, and has outputted valuable contents to the industry through case insight. By now, Tiger Roar Award has been successfully held for 13 sessions. Back to Sohu to View More
snippets.subject: Tiger Roar Awards
snippets.reasoning: Key facts: 600+ expert jury members, 1% gold award winning rate, 13 sessions. Establishes the selectivity and longevity of the awards program.
